title: ML OCR fallback reads OLLAMA_URL with silent default, bypassing the spec 043 ENABLE_OLLAMA SST gate

scenarios.title: handle_ocr_request reads ENABLE_OLLAMA fail-loud (no positional default)
scenarios.given: ml/app/ocr.py handle_ocr_request reaches the slow path (Tesseract output < MIN_TESSERACT_CHARS)
scenarios.when: the function consults the OCR-fallback gate
scenarios.then: it reads `os.environ["ENABLE_OLLAMA"]` (raises KeyError when missing) and does NOT call `os.environ.get(KEY, default)` or `os.getenv(KEY, default)` for ENABLE_OLLAMA or any other gate variable
scenarios.specSource: ml/app/ocr.py line 235 (pre-fix had `os.environ.get("OLLAMA_URL", "")` silent-default; post-fix has `os.environ["ENABLE_OLLAMA"]` fail-loud)
scenarios.adversarial: YES — fails RED if `os.environ["ENABLE_OLLAMA"]` is replaced with positional default

scenarios.title: ENABLE_OLLAMA truthy invokes Ollama fallback
scenarios.given: ENABLE_OLLAMA is set to one of `true`/`1`/`yes`/`on` (case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed) and Tesseract output is shorter than MIN_TESSERACT_CHARS
scenarios.then: extract_text_ollama is invoked and the returned ocr_engine field equals `ollama` if Ollama returned a longer result than Tesseract
scenarios.specSource: specs/043-ollama-test-infrastructure/spec.md line 331 + design.md line 91 (per-env ENABLE_OLLAMA SST flag controls Ollama fallback gating)
scenarios.adversarial: YES — fails RED if pre-fix call site shape (positional `ollama_url`) is restored

scenarios.title: ENABLE_OLLAMA falsy skips Ollama fallback
scenarios.given: ENABLE_OLLAMA is set to one of `false`/`0`/`no`/`off`/`""` (case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed) and Tesseract output is shorter than MIN_TESSERACT_CHARS
scenarios.then: extract_text_ollama is NOT invoked and the returned ocr_engine field equals `tesseract` with the original Tesseract result text
scenarios.specSource: specs/043-ollama-test-infrastructure/design.md line 91 (per-env ENABLE_OLLAMA defaults to false for dev and home-lab)
scenarios.adversarial: NO (positive guard rail — passes on both pre-fix and post-fix; locks the behavior contract going forward)

scenarios.title: ENABLE_OLLAMA missing raises KeyError on the slow path
scenarios.given: ENABLE_OLLAMA is not present in the environment and Tesseract output is shorter than MIN_TESSERACT_CHARS
scenarios.specSource: .github/instructions/smackerel-no-defaults.instructions.md (Gate G028 — `os.getenv(KEY, default)` and `os.environ.get(KEY, default)` are explicitly banned in Python)
scenarios.adversarial: YES — fails RED if production code has any defensive default for ENABLE_OLLAMA

scenarios.title: ENABLE_OLLAMA invalid value raises RuntimeError naming Gate G028
scenarios.given: ENABLE_OLLAMA is set to a non-canonical token (e.g. `maybe`, `enabled`, `disabled`) and Tesseract output is shorter than MIN_TESSERACT_CHARS
scenarios.then: it raises RuntimeError mentioning `ENABLE_OLLAMA must be exactly one of` AND naming the invalid value
scenarios.specSource: design.md DD-3 (strict tokens, no defensive coercion) + .github/instructions/smackerel-no-defaults.instructions.md (Gate G028)
scenarios.adversarial: YES — fails RED if production code has no validation block

scenarios.title: ENABLE_OLLAMA only consulted on the slow path (lazy gating)
scenarios.given: Tesseract output is sufficient (>= MIN_TESSERACT_CHARS) and ENABLE_OLLAMA is NOT present in the environment (which would raise KeyError if read)
scenarios.then: handle_ocr_request returns successfully with ocr_engine=`tesseract` and ENABLE_OLLAMA is never read (no KeyError raised)
scenarios.specSource: design.md DD-2 (lazy-read on the slow path only)
scenarios.adversarial: NO (positive guard rail — proves the gate is not hoisted to module load or to the top of handle_ocr_request)
